**About the Opportunity**

**Job Summary**
**The Director of Major Gifts, Khoury College, is the content specialist responsible for building and executing Khoury College’s fundraising strategy. Reporting to the Executive Director, Interdisciplinary Advancement, this role works with faculty at the College and Advancement staff across the university to discover, identify, and build relationships with prospective donors, developing strategies for their cultivation and soliciting gifts. The Director plans, organizes, and carries out Khoury’s major gifts fundraising program for alumni, parents, and friends of the College; manages a portfolio of 120\+ prospective donors; and solicits gifts in the $100K–$1M\+ range. The position identifies key regions nationally for cultivating and soliciting donors and collaborates with Advancement colleagues across multiple functions. This position is Boston\-based and is not available for remote work.**


**Key Responsibilities \& Accountabilities**
============================================

**Major Gifts Fundraising Leadership**

* Lead the major gifts fundraising effort and serve as the college’s content expert for Advancement
* Cultivate, solicit, and build relationships with alumni, parents, and friends
* Strategize with faculty and colleagues on next steps for prospects across the college and disciplines
* Solicit gifts in the $100K–$1M\+ range
* Advise faculty, fundraising colleagues, and administrators on alumni and parent areas of interest
* Partner with Advancement teams, including corporate and foundation relations, leadership giving, international and regional giving, gift planning, and family philanthropy, to develop interdisciplinary donor strategies
* Travel as needed to build relationships with volunteers, prospects, and donors

**Prospect Portfolio and Pipeline Management**

* Discover new prospects and build a pipeline to support major gift fundraising
* Develop and manage a portfolio of approximately 120\+ prospects
* Establish region\-based plans for cultivating and soliciting alumni and parents in targeted areas nationally
* Engage faculty strategically with prospects and maintain ongoing correspondence
* Keep leadership and stakeholders informed of funding opportunities and trends
* Travel to strategically identified regions to support prospect identification, cultivation, solicitation, and stewardship
* Achieve annual fundraising and activity metrics set by the Executive Director

**Prospect Portfolio Maintenance and Reporting**

* Maintain a high\-quality development process for active alumni, parent, and friend prospects and donors
* Create call reports and maintain database records using Development Office systems (Ascend)
* Ensure data integrity of the prospect portfolio
* Prepare monthly prospect progress reports for the Executive Director
Partner with the Stewardship team to ensure donors are appropriately recognized and supported  
* 

  

**Minimum Qualifications**
==========================

**Required**

* Bachelor’s degree
* 6–8 years of professional experience, including at least 5 years of demonstrated success in soliciting and closing six\- and seven\-figure gifts in support of a nonprofit institution
* Demonstrated experience cultivating and soliciting alumni, parents, and friends, and maintaining positive donor relationships
* Strong communication and interpersonal skills with the ability to build relationships with internal and external stakeholders
* Ability to work independently and collaboratively within a university Advancement team
* Data\- and metric\-driven mindset
* Appreciation and understanding of the mission of Northeastern University
* Ability to travel overnight and work evenings and weekends as needed

**Preferred**

* Familiarity with the technology industry
STEM or computer science\-related educational background

Working at Yale means contributing to a better tomorrow. Reporting to the director of communications, the assistant director of communications will support external and internal communications for the Office of the President. new_jobs.title: Executive Director of Enrollment Management and Marketing
new_jobs.description:
* Full Time

**Position Summary**



Antioch College seeks an innovative, strategic, and entrepreneurial leader to serve as our Executive Director of Enrollment Management and Marketing.



Reporting to the President, the Executive Director provides strategic leadership for all aspects of enrollment management, admissions, financial aid, and marketing. This role is responsible for developing and executing a comprehensive enrollment plan that supports institutional goals for enrollment growth, retention, and net tuition revenue, while authentically showcasing Antioch’s distinctive interdisciplinary curriculum, cooperative education model, and Federal Work College identity.



The Executive Director will lead a collaborative team and serve as a key member of the President’s Cabinet, partnering closely with Academic Affairs, Student Life, and Advancement to align enrollment efforts with Antioch’s values and strengths.


**Key Responsibilities**


* Develop, implement, and assess a data\-driven, multi\-year strategic enrollment management plan, including recruitment, admissions, yield, and retention initiatives
* Oversee integrated marketing and external communications efforts, including digital campaigns, social media, and website content, to amplify Antioch’s brand at regional and national levels.
* Collaborate with the Admissions team in executing effective recruitment strategies, such as campus visits, high school outreach, college fairs, virtual programming, and relationship\-building with counselors, students, and families.
* Manage financial aid strategies to maximize access, affordability, and net tuition revenue while maintaining academic quality and diversity.
* Utilize enrollment data and institutional research to inform decision\-making, forecast trends, and report on key metrics to senior leadership and the Board of Trustees.
* Collaborate with faculty, staff, and the cooperative education / campus work program team to ensure marketing and recruitment materials effectively communicate the value of our distinctive educational model.
* Foster a student\-centered, inclusive approach that supports diversity, equity, and access.
* Manage the enrollment budget, staff development, and operations for efficiency and excellence.
* Represent the college externally and build strong partnerships with key stakeholders.


**We seek a leader who:**


* Understands the changing landscape of higher education.
* Thrives in entrepreneurial and mission\-driven environments.
* Possesses strong analytical and financial acumen.
* Values innovation, collaboration, and accountability.
* Believes deeply in the transformative power of applied liberal arts education.


At Antioch, you will have the opportunity to help design the future of higher education while advancing a mission dedicated to helping students “win victories for humanity.”


**Qualifications**


* Master’s or terminal degree preferred.
* Significant progressive leadership experience (5\+ years) in enrollment management, admissions, or related higher education areas.
* Demonstrated success in developing and achieving enrollment goals, including experience with marketing, digital recruitment, and financial aid strategies.
* Strong understanding of current trends in higher education enrollment, particularly for small, private, or distinctive institutions.
* Excellent leadership, communication, interpersonal, and analytical skills with a collaborative, team\-oriented approach.
* Track record of successful team building, budget management, and cross\-divisional collaboration.
* Proficiency with enrollment technologies, data analytics, and a commitment to diversity, equity, inclusion, and belonging


**About Antioch College**



Antioch College was founded as a new kind of American college, a place where students seek to “win victories for humanity” and engage in real\-world problem solving in the classroom, campus, and community. As a Federal Work College with a renowned cooperative education program, meaningful work is integral to the Antioch experience. Guided by talented faculty and advisors, students own their education by creating self\-designed majors through one of five interdisciplinary pathways.



Antioch College embraces diversity as a core value, and considers it fundamental to excellence in education. We are an education community that believes that scholarship and life experience are strengthened when linked. We seek candidates who will authentically engage our community in the pursuit of winning victories for humanities.



Located in Yellow Springs, Ohio, Antioch College is a nationally recognized liberal arts college grounded in experiential learning, cooperative education, social justice, and community engagement. Antioch offers students a distinctive educational experience that integrates interdisciplinary inquiry, meaningful work, and real\-world impact. Antioch occupies a unique place in the landscape of higher education as the tenth Federal Work College in the nation and the first in Ohio; as a pioneer in cooperative education; as a *College that Changes Lives*; and as a nationally recognized inclusive community.



This is an extraordinary opportunity for a visionary enrollment leader who sees the challenges facing higher education as opportunities for innovation.

* A cover letter addressing relevant experience and interest in the position.
